thread.title: Haryana Human Rights Commission Set to Reopen After 14-Month Wait for New Appointments | Chandigarh News - Times of India
thread.title_full: Haryana Human Rights Commission Set to Reopen After 14-Month Wait for New Appointments | Chandigarh News - Times of India

author: Manvir Singh Saini
title: Haryana Human Rights Commission Set to Reopen After 14-Month Wait for New Appointments | Chandigarh News - Times of India
text: Chandigarh: After a wait of 14 months, the Haryana Human Rights Commission (HHRC) is all set get a chairperson and two members as an empowered committee of the govt headed by chief minister Nayab Singh Saini has reportedly finalised the names for the rights body. In the absence of members and chairperson, the work of the commission had come to a standstill and complaints had been piling up in its office here.115612072 As per information, CM Nayab Singh Saini, additional chief secretary (home) Anurag Rastogi and Haryana assembly speaker Harvinder Kalyan on Saturday met to discuss and finalise the names. Notably, the Haryana Human Rights Act provides for the leader of opposition as one of the members in the selection of the chairperson and the members of the commission. But since the name of the LoP has not been finalised so far, the meeting was carried out without the leader of the opposition.Sources said the committee which met on Saturday also ascertained whether they could proceed without the LoP; and after studying the Act, the committee went ahead with its move to finalise the names.After finalising three names, the file has been forwarded to Haryana governor Bandaru Dattatreya for final appointment and notification, the sources added.Initially, the commission was represented by chairperson Justice K C Puri (retd) and member Deep Bhatia. While Justice Puri held the office till April 2023, Bhatia served as the only member and officiated as the head of the body till his tenure ended in Sept 2023. A Kaithal resident then moved the Punjab and Haryana high court and following its directions, the Haryana govt had assured the court of filling the vacancies. We also published the following articles recentlyIndia name 20-member team for men's Junior Asia CupIndia is set to compete in the Men's Junior Asia Cup hockey tournament in Muscat, aiming for their fifth title. The tournament, starting November 26th, doubles as a qualifier for the Junior World Cup.115408064Polling held for the first time to elect members to 3 assembly committeesIn an unprecedented event, Andhra Pradesh assembly witnessed its first-ever election for committee positions. This unusual situation arose due to the YSRCP, despite lacking opposition status, fielding candidates for the Public Accounts Committee (PAC), Public Estimates Committee, and Public Undertakings Committee.115574365Polling held for the first time to elect members to 3 assembly committeesIn an unprecedented move, Andhra Pradesh assembly witnessed elections for three key committees - PAC, PEC, and PUC - after the ruling NDA filed nominations for all positions. This decision came as the sole opposition party, YSRCP, boycotted the assembly session. Despite the boycott, YSRCP filed individual nominations for each committee.115574366
